title: consolidate Makefile validation/tools installs
body:
Create a Makefile in the root of the repo to consolidate how we install
golangci-lint and git-validation, go-md2man. Installing codespell is left
to the user as it is a python program and I didn't want to depend on a
pip install and it wasn't installed anywhere previously either.

Then remove a bunch of targets in the common, image, storage Makefiles
that are done now by the main Makefile.

With that we can also get rid of the custom tools vendor dir in storage.

---
Makefiles: consolidate go vendor targets

First, we don't do vendoring here so rename the targets to tidy. Then
use the same logic in each target, run tidy, verify and remove any
possible toolchain version as we don't want that.

comments.body:
>   failed to fetch pull request patch: RequestError [HttpError]: Sorry, the diff exceeded the maximum number of files (300). Consider using 'List pull requests files' API or locally cloning the repository instead.: {"resource":"PullRequest","field":"diff","code":"too_large"} - https://docs.github.com/rest/pulls/pulls#list-pull-requests-files


Looks like the big diff due the vendor removal breaks the -extra linter task for common as this only lints new issues, but as it failed to load the diff it seems like it lints everything instead.

Does anyone see any value in having a new issue only lint? I think it would be much simpler to just fix the existing lints once and then not having to deal with two runs.

@containers/container-libs-maintainers PTAL

I am far of a Makefile expert so any comments or suggestions how to improve things are welcome.
I can also split out the golangci-lint change if wanted
